text:
Stakeout × Preparation × Battle Start (張り込み×準備×血戦開始, Harikomu × Junbi × Chisen Kaishi) is the 88 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 10 th episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on June 2 nd , 2004.

Overview

Gon, Killua, and Biscuit receive updates on Tsezguerra's disappearance from Goreinu, who diligently monitors Genthru's team. He elaborates on the arrangements made with Tsezguerra, providing critical context to their situation. Meanwhile, Tsezguerra and his group learn from Battera that he has dismissed all participants and ceased the Greed Island mission due to the recent passing of his girlfriend. In response to being pursued by Genthru's team, Gon, Killua, and Biscuit formulate a strategic plan to isolate their adversaries, aiming to confront them individually in combat.

Synopsis

Battera sits on a couch, tears streaming down his face, watching a clip of himself with a beautiful young woman. As Tsezguerra and his team search the empty, abandoned building, they eventually find him. Concerned, Tsezguerra asks what happened. With a sorrowful tone, Battera explains that all the participants he hired have been dismissed. Disappointed, Tsezguerra presses for more details, eager to understand Battera’s situation. However, Battera reveals that he no longer needs any cards and that the team can keep whatever cards they have as compensation for their efforts. He assures them that his lawyer will handle their payment. Genthru, Bara, and Sub arrive at Battera’s place in their pursuit of Tsezguerra’s team.

Back to Gon, Killua, and Bisuit. Gon begins his training by focusing on his Nen while Killua and Biscuit discuss Tsezguerra and his team, who have been missing from the game for days and worried that their cards might vanish. With no further information about what happened to Tsezguerra’s group, Killua decides to wait for a message from Goreinu to learn more.

Meanwhile, Gon completes his training and happily shares his progress with Killua and Biscuit, eager to continue practicing his Emission-type training. However, Biscuit disagrees, insisting he continue with his current training. Just then, Goreinu initiates Contact with them.

From a distance, Goreinu watches Genthru and his team from a tree while waiting for Tsezguerra to emerge. He updates Biscuit and Killua through contact, explaining that Tsezguerra's group hasn’t returned to the game and that they can only come back once Goreinu sends a message indicating it’s safe. Killua and Biscuit remind him that their card data will vanish if they stay away too long. Goreinu assures them that he holds all the important cards, which are part of the arrangement with Tsezguerra. This worries Killua further, as he realizes that if Genthru discovers they do not hold the important cards, it could create significant problems.

Meanwhile, after watching Genthru's team head to Masadora in the distance, Goreinu informs Killua and the others about the situation. He explains why they shouldn’t worry about the cards, clarifying the exchange of fake and important cards involving Tsezguerra and mentioning that he possesses the majority of the cards. He apologizes for not being able to help them fight Genthru but reassures them that the card he wanted from the kids as part of their earlier deal can wait until after they defeat Genthru’s team. He wishes them good luck, urging them to bring justice for those killed by Genthru, which Killua confirms. Gon listened to their conversation and surprisingly perfected his training simultaneously.

Outside Greed Island, Tsezguerra, Rodriot, Kess, Barry, and Battera took a photo of a young, beautiful woman from Battera’s place and showed it to him. Tsezguerra, feeling dissatisfied, expresses his desire to know more about the situation and suspects that this woman, whom he thought was Battera's daughter or granddaughter, is linked to the unexpected events. Battera reveals that the woman is his girlfriend, who genuinely loves him; she rejects every gift he gives her and favors his handmade items, like the frame in the picture.

Battera explains that she suffered an accident and fell into a coma, and despite his best efforts and the help of professionals, they couldn’t bring her back to health. Then, Battera learned about Greed Island and its potential to contain items capable of curing any illness or injury by beating the game. This was his sole reason for hiring the team. However, after her passing, he lost the will to continue the mission, breaking their contracts.

Tsezguerra and the others agree to split the money from the contract breach, including Goreinu’s share. Nevertheless, Tsezguerra is eager to assist Gon and Killua in obtaining their remaining cards to help them deal with Genthru.

Back with Genthru’s team, after inspecting the cards possessed by Gon, Killua, and Biscuit, they devise a plan to take them down. Genthru will confront Gon, Bara will handle Biscuit, and Sub will go up against Killua. They proceed to use their Accompany card to confront the trio.

Genthru and his team arrive at the location of Gon, Killua, and Biscuit. He confronts them and proposes a deal, which Killua promptly rejects. As the trio pretends to act innocent to prevent Genthru from knowing their plan, Genthru persists in trying to make a deal, attempting to get closer to them. Gon firmly tells him to stay back and not approach them.

As Biscuit contemplates maintaining their facade, Genthru continues to negotiate, suggesting they could split the rewards and beat the game to get paid by Battera. Killua counters all of Genthru's tactics and lies, firmly refusing the deal. This provokes Genthru, causing him to act hostile and threaten the trio.

In response, they use their Accompany card to escape to Soufrabi. Genthru, aware of how many traversal cards the trio possesses, continues to pursue them.

The trio uses their last card to reach Masadora, leading Genthru and his team to suspect that they plan to escape via the port. After arriving in the woods, Gon, Killua, and Biscuit hide while observing Genthru's group. Genthru and his team begin searching for them in the surrounding areas.

After searching without success, Genthru’s group decides to use their final Accompany card to locate the trio. With both teams lacking traversal cards, Gon, Killua, and Biscuit make a run for it, pursued by Genthru’s group. Eventually, Genthru’s team corners the trio. Genthru threatens to give them the cards, but Gon furiously declines. Biscuit realizes that Genthru's strategy involves confronting them one by one.

As Genthru’s team continues to mock the trio, Killua decides to set their plan in motion by separating them. He sprints away, prompting Sub to follow and engage him. Despite stating that his hands aren't fully healed, Killua believes they are good enough to be useful and tells Biscuit to run. He distances himself from the group, leading Sub in pursuit, while Biscuit also flees, with Bara following her. This leaves Gon to face Genthru alone.

Genthru makes his move, reaching for Gon, but Gon swiftly taps Genthru's hand away and dodges his follow-up attack. This alerts Genthru, who realizes Gon knows his true identity as the Bomber. Gon informs Genthru that Tsezguerra told him about him. However, Genthru seems aware that Tsezguerra is unaware of his abilities. He threatens Gon, demanding to know who revealed his abilities, leaving Gon in tense silence as Genthru continues to intimidate him, noting that very few who are aware of his powers are still alive.
